The  Palestinian  Authority's Jerusalem Governorate said the drills were carried out in the mosque's courtyards and lasted around 30 minutes. Although only the King Faisal Gate was shut, the governorate said Israeli forces imposed heightened security measures at the complex's other entrances, disrupting worshippers' access. articles.summary: Iran's Araghchi visits Doha to pay tribute to former emir, days after attack in Qatar Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Aragchi travelled to Doha to offer condolences to Qatari officials over the death of Hamad bin Khalifa, the former emir and father of the current ruler Tamim Al Thani. Sheikh Hamad’s death was announced on Sunday, the same day in which Iran attacked what it said were US targets in Qatar. Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said it had targeted Qatar's Al Udeid, the biggest US base in the Middle East, with ballistic missiles.

articles.title: Israeli soldier sentenced for sending videos of missile interceptions to Iranian agent
articles.summary: Israeli soldier sentenced for sending videos of missile interceptions to Iranian agent Israel's military court sentenced an active-duty soldier to five years in prison for sending videos of missile interceptions to an Iranian agent, the Israeli army announced. The soldier was accused of passing sensitive information via Telegram to the agents, in exchange for financial compensation. The soldier voluntarily confessed to someone in his military unit that he had been collaborating with foreign agents, according to the Israeli military’s statement, leading to his arrest by the Shin Bet.
